case_type_note: This case file intentionally contains NO ground_truth verdict. The rubric scores the analysis on framework application, not against a pre-known answer. Use this case to test whether the analysis demonstrates rigorous Moore-framework reasoning on inputs that have less retrospective public analysis in training data than iconic cases (Slack 2014, Zoom 2018).

company.name: Cursor (Anysphere)
company.description: AI-powered code editor, originally a fork of VS Code with deep AI integration. Founded 2022 by Anysphere. Position fixed at calendar Q1 2026.

current_state.revenue_arr_usd: Widely reported ARR in the to range as of late 2025 / early 2026. Exact figure varies by source. ~10x year-over-year growth from 2024.
current_state.customers_count_approx: Reported 'millions' of users on free tier. Paid users in the high hundreds of thousands. Business-tier customers: growing rapidly into mid-market engineering teams; specific named enterprise logos (Stripe, Shopify, Replicate, Anthropic itself) publicly disclosed.
current_state.customer_profile: Bottom-up developer adoption: individual engineers at startups, mid-market software companies, scale-ups, and increasingly large tech orgs. Customer base concentrated initially in Bay Area / NYC tech but global usage broad. Buyer at the team-and-up tier is increasingly the engineering manager or VP Engineering, not the IC.
current_state.sales_motion: Hybrid. Strong product-led growth via free tier and Pro (/user/month). Inside sales motion for Business tier (/user/month). Direct enterprise sales for the largest accounts, but enterprise motion is recent and still maturing. Self-serve credit card up to seat counts in the hundreds; annual contracts and procurement engagement above that.
current_state.geography_primary: United States primary. Strong international (UK, EU, India, Brazil, Japan); revenue mix not publicly disclosed but adoption patterns suggest growing international share.

expansion_thesis: Become the default AI-powered IDE for all software developers, then expand into adjacent surfaces: AI-native code review, AI-assisted refactoring at the codebase level, agent-based background coding workflows, and eventually multi-IDE / multi-language non-code creative work. Public framing: 'the AI code editor for serious software work,' positioning against GitHub Copilot (Microsoft) and Windsurf (Codeium) on quality of agentic capability, model flexibility, and developer experience.
signals: Net dollar retention in the Business tier reported above 130% — strong intra-account expansion as engineering teams standardize.
The product ships rapid model-routing updates: customers can select frontier models (Claude, GPT, Gemini variants) for different code tasks; defaults shift as new model releases land.
Free-tier dominance still significant: most signups never convert to paid; Pro tier conversion drives most individual revenue.
Engineering managers at mid-market software companies are the dominant new buyer profile in the Business tier — not VPs of IT, not procurement.
SOC 2 Type II achieved. SSO via SAML, basic admin console, audit logs available on Business and Enterprise tiers. SCIM provisioning shipped for Enterprise tier in 2025.
On-prem / VPC deployment, FedRAMP authorization, and HIPAA-aligned offerings: NOT yet generally available as of Q1 2026. Roadmapped but not shipped.

Competitive landscape intensifying: GitHub Copilot bundled with GitHub Enterprise, Microsoft pushing Copilot deeply into Visual Studio; Windsurf (Codeium) competing on the same agentic IDE shape; Continue.dev and Cline pursuing open source alternatives.

Some public concern from large enterprises about code-context-leakage to model providers; Cursor offers privacy modes and contractual data-handling commitments, but bring-your-own-model and on-prem inference requested by F500 buyers and not yet broadly delivered.
Public messaging emphasizes 'serious software work' and 'production engineering teams,' but the user base is heterogeneous from solo founders building toy apps to large eng orgs.
Anysphere has raised significant capital at premium valuations through 2025; speculation about IPO or strategic exit timing common in trade press.

Brand momentum is unusually high: the product has crossed from 'AI dev tool that some engineers love' to 'AI dev tool many engineers default to,' particularly in the AI-forward / startup-builder cohort.
notes_for_eval: This case is constructed from public reporting and trade-press signals as of early 2026. The case is intentionally NOT scored against a ground-truth verdict — there is no privileged answer to check the analysis against. The rubric in rubric.json is the sole scoring authority. A correct analysis should pick a single TALC stage and chasm position decisively, recommend a beachhead at role-level specificity, name concrete whole-product gaps, sequence bowling pins with reference-value rationale, and flag failure modes only where the input evidence supports them. Reasonable analysts may disagree on whether Cursor is 'across the chasm with regulated-industry pins ahead' or 'in the chasm with whole-product gaps blocking F500.' Both positions are defensible if the reasoning is rigorous and tied to the inputs. Disagreement on the conclusion does not penalize an analysis; weak framework application does.
